edition: Ānandāśrama Sanskrit Series No. 13, *Taittirīya-Upaniṣad-Vārttika-Bhāṣya with Tīkā by Sureśvarācārya*, Pune, 1889

expected_text_units.source_for_estimate: Sureśvara's TUBV has roughly 1100 vārttikas across the three vallīs of the Taittirīya

ground_truth_source: R. Balasubramanian, *The Taittirīyopaniṣad-Bhāṣya-Vārttika of Sureśvara* (Madras 1974) — full English with Sanskrit base; PDF at archive.org `taittiriyavartika` and centreforbrahmavidya.org.
first_page_visual_quality_notes: Heuristic (KB/page ~906): exceptional high-DPI scan. Producer 'BookChanger 4.6.3'. 225 pages.
ocr_friendliness_notes: Three-text Anandashram layout (Śaṅkara base + Sureśvara vārttika + Ānandagiri ṭīkā) is the same region-segmentation challenge as BUBV, but a much smaller corpus and a much cleaner scan. Better OCR development target than BUBV.
ia_filename: Taitiriya Upanishad Bhashya Vartika by Sureshvara Acharya 1889 - Anand Ashram Press.pdf
